text: Title : Pelaksanaan Tradisi Dikili / Ma ulid dalam Konteks Kebudayaan dan Agama Masyarakat Gorontalo The tradition of dikili or ma ulid in Islamic celebration of the birth of the Prophet Muhammad has become a central theme of this study. This study basically employ qualitative approach method with empirical and library research on how Islamic aesthetic norms and Gorontalo culture tradition integrate into each other in celebration of the Prophet Muhammad’s birth. The results of the study show thatdikili or ma ulid becomes an important practice of literary and religious character, where it is expressed in the form of collective remembering and memorizing verses of the Qur’an in order to educate the population towards religious values and moral values. This tradition in Gorontalo has a specific form of celebration as articulated in the verses of syair and the traditional food known as walimah or ornamental food or symbol of the community's thanksgiving and optimism for attainment of prosperity. The celebration of dikili / ma ulid is further expounded by Gorontalo people on the principle of adati hula-hula- hula-‘a to sara-‘a, sara-‘a hula- hula-‘a to Kuru’ani, which implies that Gorontalo customs are basically based on the Shariah Islamic law and the Qur’an. This study concludes that modikili as a customary celebration in Gorontalo has a meaning of great values, where by this celebration is seen as the implementation of Islamic ideology in Gorontalo society in order to maintain Islamic identities while at the same time as to strengthen solidarity among members of Gorontalo society through the common ancestor cult tradition.
